description_long: This object is a vintage television with a rectangular wooden cabinet featuring a rich, reddish-brown wood grain finish. The front face displays a large, slightly curved glass screen bordered by a thick wooden bezel, with a black frame surrounding the display area. To the right of the screen, there are two prominent rotary dials labeled for channel and volume control, as well as a perforated speaker grille below them. The back of the television protrudes outward in a trapezoidal shape, made of black plastic or metal, likely housing the cathode ray tube and ventilation holes. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the classic boxy silhouette typical of mid-to-late 20th-century televisions. This design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for placement in living rooms, garages, or even as discarded street furniture in urban environments.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a wood-grain cabinet, large glass screen, rotary dials, and a protruding black back panel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the smooth, reddish-brown wooden surface with rounded edges and the black back panel slightly visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the protruding black back panel with ventilation holes, set against the wooden cabinet.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the uninterrupted wooden surface and the edge of the black back panel.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the large glass screen, two rotary dials for channel and volume, and a perforated speaker grille on the right.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, wooden bezel with a reddish-brown finish and rounded corners.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two rotary dials and a speaker grille on the front right panel, arranged vertically.
